title: DSDS Anatomy Guideline
description: Documents the visual structure of a component or pattern — its named sub-elements (parts), their associated design tokens, and optional visual previews. Anatomy guidelines decompose an artifact into the parts that designers and engineers need to understand, style, and test. For components, parts are rendered UI sub-elements (container, label, icon). For patterns, parts are sections of the pattern's visual layout (image, title, body, primary action).

anatomyEntry.description: A named sub-element of a component's or pattern's visual structure.

description.description: What this part is, what it does, and any constraints on its content or appearance.

required.description: Whether this part is always present in the rendered output. Defaults to false. When false, the part is conditionally rendered based on props or content.

tokens.description: Design tokens applied to this part. Keys are token-purpose names describing what visual attribute the token controls (e.g., 'background', 'border-radius', 'padding-horizontal', 'text-color'). Values are the token names that provide the value (e.g., 'color-action-primary', 'radius-md', 'space-4').

links.description: Links to resources for this anatomy part — design tool nodes, source code blocks, documentation sections, or other addressable references.

anatomy.description: Documents the visual structure of a component or pattern by listing its named sub-elements (parts). Each part can reference the design tokens that style it, bridging visual design and token architecture. An optional preview shows an annotated diagram of the assembled structure. For components, parts are rendered UI sub-elements (e.g., container, label, icon, focus-ring). For patterns, parts are structural sections of the layout (e.g., image, title, body, primary action).

kind.description: Identifies this guideline as an anatomy spec.

description.description: Prose overview of the artifact's visual structure — how the parts relate and any notable structural constraints.

parts.description: The named sub-elements of the artifact, in visual order (typically outside-in or top-to-bottom). Each part documents an element that can be styled, tested, or referenced independently.

preview.description: Visual previews of the anatomy — typically annotated diagrams with numbered callouts mapping to the parts array. Multiple previews allow different views (e.g., expanded vs. collapsed, with icon vs. without).
